builds.description: A personal stylist for women rebuilding their wardrobe after pregnancy. Five questions about body changes and lifestyle return a 12-piece capsule shopping list with direct purchase links.
An AI practice partner for Carnatic vocal students. Listens to your daily riyaaz, flags pitch drift between guru sessions, and gives one specific thing to work on tomorrow.
A weekly meal planner for South Indian home cooks who default to the same five dishes. Builds a seven-day plan around what's already in your kitchen and what's seasonal at the local market.
A five-minute journaling companion for first-time parents in the early postpartum months. Three prompts a day, no streaks, no pressure — just a record of how the first year actually felt.
A spoken English coach for working professionals preparing for client calls. Roleplays the scenario, flags filler words and unclear phrasing, gives you a short script before the meeting.
A budget tracker built around irregular freelancer income cycles. Smooths feast-and-famine months into a steady drawable allowance and warns you before the lean weeks bite.
A dietary tracker for type-2 diabetics built around Indian home meals. Logs photos of your thali, estimates the carb load, and suggests one small swap for the next meal.
A resume rewriter for mid-career professionals making lateral switches. Translates your last role into the language of the role you actually want and produces three tailored versions in five minutes.
A wedding planning copilot for couples managing parents and vendors in parallel. Holds the Google Sheet, the WhatsApp threads, and the family expectations in one place so nothing falls through.
